Boston demolition plan : environmental impact statement.

The City of Boston proposed a plan to demolish 900 abandoned and unsafe residential buildings over two years. This initiative, part of the Community Development Block Grant Program, aims to eliminate fire, safety, and health hazards. The demolition will occur in seven areas: Roxbury, Dorchester, Jamaica Plain, South Boston, Franklin Field, Upham's Corner, and Mattapan. The plan is intended to improve neighborhood safety and aesthetics, but it may also cause temporary traffic congestion, increased dust, and noise. Alternatives like rehabilitation and boarding were considered but deemed less effective.
City of Boston Office of Community Development

The Boston demolition plan was proposed to address the significant safety and health hazards posed by 900 abandoned residential buildings throughout the city. This initiative was part of a broader effort to revitalize neighborhoods and improve living conditions, particularly in areas that had been neglected. While there was some public concern regarding potential disruptions during the demolition process, the overall sentiment leaned towards support for the project due to its potential benefits. Ultimately, the plan aimed to enhance community safety and aesthetics, and it was executed as part of the Community Development Block Grant Program.
United States. Department of Housing and Urban Development
